paper_title: Gaussian Processes for time-marked time-series data

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Initial Paper Discovery and Constraint Filtering
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: read the documents carefully and find at least 5 - 8 papers with a small description and working link and paper pdf , reproducability , prerequisistis
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped narrow down thousands of papers to 7 candidates that strictly fit the CORE A* (2009-2012) and classical ML method constraints.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the CORE portal (https://portal.core.edu.au/conf-ranks/) for AISTATS and confirmed the paper 'Gaussian Processes for Time-Marked Time-Series Data' was published in 2012 as required.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: is it practical to choose this paper or it is very rigourous
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Balanced the high mathematical rigor of GPs against the practical implementation ease using CPU-only libraries like scikit-learn.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed that the paper focuses on methodological contributions rather than heavy compute, making it safe for my personal laptop.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: explain me the pros and cons of choosing this
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified the synergy with Unit 1 (Time Series) which is valuable for the 60% weightage Part D case studies.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-referenced the Units listed in the Midsem Instructions (Time Series and SVM) to ensure the paper covers both.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: how much minimum no. of hours should I give to it to understand the depth , I am an average student
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Established a 20-30 hour roadmap to ensure I take 'deep ownership' of the paper before the proctored sessions.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I mapped this to the 10+ day distribution for Part B provided in the exam guidelines and found it to be a viable schedule.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: explain the math in the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Simplified the transition from continuous GP latent states to discrete Poisson event marks using the log-intensity linking function.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I verified the log-intensity concept (lambda = exp(f)) against general Gaussian Process regression literature and class notes.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: can i use the dataset / smaller version of the dataset used in the actual paper
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that using simplified experimental setups or toy datasets is permitted as long as the 50-point time-series minimum is met.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked Section 4 (Part A) of the instructions which confirms that toy datasets or simplified setups are allowed.

top_5_prompts.prompt: is it practical to choose this paper or it is very rigourous
top_5_prompts.why_important: This helped me decide if the paper's difficulty was manageable for an average student under proctored conditions.

top_5_prompts.prompt: explain the math in the paper
top_5_prompts.why_important: Essential for understanding the 'design choices' and 'assumptions' I will have to justify in Part C without internet access.

top_5_prompts.prompt: explain me the pros and cons of choosing this
top_5_prompts.why_important: Identified the alignment with Part D syllabus units, which carry the largest portion (60%) of the mid-sem marks.

top_5_prompts.prompt: read the documents carefully and find at least 5 - 8 papers with a small description and working paper pdf , reproducability , prerequisistis
top_5_prompts.why_important: Primary prompt used to ensure the paper selected fits the strict CORE A* and 2009-2012 year constraints.

top_5_prompts.prompt: can i use the dataset / smaller version of the dataset used in the actual paper
top_5_prompts.why_important: Confirmed that I can run experiments on a standard CPU-only laptop by simplifying the data scope.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A.

details.verification: Declaration: True, Acknowledged: True, Top5: True

details.prompts_depth: Prompts are mostly superficial requests for explanations and basic feasibility checks. Only prompt 3 shows slight analytical thinking about pros/cons, but lacks depth about failure modes, trade-offs, or technical limitations.
details.prompts_relevance: All prompts directly relate to paper selection feasibility - assessing difficulty, understanding requirements, checking constraints, and confirming reproducibility on available hardware. Highly relevant to the selection process.
